prose:
The fukui reactivity indices for nitrobenzene have been computed using Density Functional Theory (DFT) with the B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set (tier `standard`). The calculation was performed in the gas phase, with a charge of 0 and a multiplicity of 1. The geometry was taken from the user-provided `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz` file.

The Fukui indices are as follows:

| Atom Index (0-based) | Atom Label (1-based) | Symbol | Fukui f+ (Electrophilic) | Fukui f- (Nucleophilic) | Fukui f0 (Radical) |
|:---------------------|:---------------------|:-------|:-------------------------|:------------------------|:-------------------|
| 0                    | 1                    | O      | 0.47317                  | 0.09702                 | 0.28509            |
| 1                    | 2                    | O      | 0.47344                  | 0.09677                 | 0.28511            |
| 2                    | 3                    | N      | -0.45670                 | 0.01724                 | -0.21973           |
| 3                    | 4                    | C      | 0.49642                  | -0.01429                | 0.24106            |
| 4                    | 5                    | C      | 0.11563                  | 0.10729                 | 0.11146            |
| 5                    | 6                    | C      | 0.11588                  | 0.10724                 | 0.11156            |
| 6                    | 7                    | C      | 0.17412                  | 0.09492                 | 0.13452            |
| 7                    | 8                    | C      | 0.17423                  | 0.09489                 | 0.13456            |
| 8                    | 9                    | C      | 0.28914                  | 0.00831                 | 0.14872            |
| 9                    | 10                   | H      | -0.23169                 | 0.07521                 | -0.07824           |
| 10                   | 11                   | H      | -0.23182                 | 0.07520                 | -0.07831           |
| 11                   | 12                   | H      | -0.13372                 | 0.08388                 | -0.02492           |
| 12                   | 13                   | H      | -0.13379                 | 0.08388                 | -0.02496           |
| 13                   | 14                   | H      | -0.12431                 | 0.07243                 | -0.02594           |

The most electrophilic atom (highest f+) is Carbon at index 3 (label 4) with f+ = 0.49642.
The most nucleophilic atom (highest f-) is Carbon at index 4 (label 5) with f- = 0.10729.

The calculation log is available live at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/argo_gemini-2.5-flash/20260703-193456_nitrobenzene_fukui_reactivity/fukui_20260703-193511.out`.
